1: And hearken to all my words.
2: My tongue hath spoken in my mouth.
3: And my lips shall utter knowledge clearly.
4: And the breath of the Almighty hath given me life.
5: Set thy words thy words in order before me, stand up.
6: I also am formed out of the clay.
7: Neither shall my hand be heavy upon thee.
8: And I have heard the voice of thy thy words, saying saying ,
9: I am am innocent; neither is there is there iniquity in me.
10: He counteth me for his enemy,
12: that God is greater than man.
13: For he giveth not account of any of his matters.
14: Yea twice, yet man yet man perceiveth it not.
18: And his life from perishing by the sword.
19: And the multitude of his bones with strong pain: pain:
21: And his bones that that were not seen stick out.
22: And his life to the destroyers.
23: To shew unto man his uprightness:
25: He shall return to the days of his youth:
26: For he will render unto man his righteousness.
28: And his life shall see the light.
30: To be enlightened with the light of the living.
31: Hold thy peace, and I will speak.
32: Speak, for I desire to justify thee.
33: Hold thy peace, and I shall teach thee wisdom.
